Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails with a query related to "QC exceptions" or "Tyler-cleared" or "Tyler Washington" or "spot check" (or similar) using search_emails (folder_name: "SENT") to retrieve Raymond's original March 28 QC exception summary email_email_76f6a910dc8e from raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com to grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com, subject "QC exceptions found on 3 Tyler-cleared files", body listing LN-2026-00603 (Heather Sullivan, closed), LN-2026-00211 (open), and LN-2026-00224 (open).
OE 2: Search emails with a query related to "Next steps after Tyler meeting" or "exception detail by file" (or similar) using search_emails (folder_name: "INBOX") to retrieve Grace's reply email_email_ccc2b437ae19 from grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com to denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com and raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com containing the three follow-up buckets the corrected writeup must answer: (1) exposure on the closed file, (2) whether the other two files can be remediated cleanly, and (3) whether this was isolated misconduct or a normalized process/training failure.
OE 3: Search Slack #compliance-alerts using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "spot checks" or "docs came in late" or similar, filter_in_channel: "C004", filter_users_from: "keystone_a989261d4d33") to find Denise Holloway's 2026-04-07 16:13 message about doing spot checks closer to closing on loans where docs came in late or changed after initial review -the audit-pressure context Raymond's prompt references and the tone anchor for the heads-up post.
OE 4: Pull the LOS record for LN-2026-00603 using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00603") to discover id=los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f, status="processing" (NOT "closed" as Raymond's email said), loan_type="conventional", loan_amount=260000, borrower_id="los_borrower_7be15a85117e", assigned_processor="los_staff_266153ea2738", property_address="6643 Rea Rd, Charlotte, NC 28211", rate_lock_expiration="2026-04-26", closing_date=null.
OE 5: Pull the borrower record on LN-2026-00603 using mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id (borrower_id: "los_borrower_7be15a85117e") to confirm first_name="Janet", last_name="Torres", email="janet.torres.603@gmail.com", phone="(704) 267-4456", address="9392 Rocky River Rd, Charlotte, NC 28216", credit_score=700, annual_income=105000 -the borrower of record is Janet Torres, NOT Heather Sullivan as Raymond's March 28 email claimed.
OE 6: Pull the underwriting condition list for LN-2026-00603 using mortgage_los_get_conditions (loan_id: "los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f") to discover an empty list -no condition records exist for this loan in the LOS, which means the "cleared without docs" framing in Raymond's original email cannot be validated against any structured condition record.
OE 7: Pull the document checklist for LN-2026-00603 using mortgage_los_get_documents (loan_id: "los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f") to discover 14 checklist items, with tax_return_prior in status="approved" with received_date=null (the anomalous "approved without received" pattern), urla and paystub_1 still status="required", and 11 items received on 2026-03-10.
OE 8: Pull the LOS activity log for LN-2026-00603 using mortgage_los_get_activity_log (loan_id: "los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f") to discover an empty list -no activity log entries exist for this loan, so the Tyler Washington attribution lives only in Raymond's narrative email, not in any auditable LOS trail.
OE 9: Pull the LOS record for LN-2026-00211 using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00211") to discover id=los_loan_223826af9a5d, status="withdrawn" (NOT "open" as Raymond's email said), loan_type="fha", loan_amount=217000, borrower_id="los_borrower_d05b0b87b598", assigned_processor="los_staff_afc9caafae9d", property_address="5049 Brookshire Blvd, Charlotte, NC 28211", closing_date=null.
OE 10: Pull the borrower record on LN-2026-00211 using mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id (borrower_id: "los_borrower_d05b0b87b598") to confirm first_name="Elizabeth", last_name="Gray", email="elizabeth.gray.211@gmail.com", credit_score=643, annual_income=119000.
OE 11: Pull the underwriting condition list for LN-2026-00211 using mortgage_los_get_conditions (loan_id: "los_loan_223826af9a5d") to discover an empty list -no condition records in the LOS for this loan.
OE 12: Pull the document checklist for LN-2026-00211 using mortgage_los_get_documents (loan_id: "los_loan_223826af9a5d") to discover 14 items, with w2_prior and bank_statement_2 still status="required" (missing) and other items received on 2026-03-10.
OE 13: Pull the LOS activity log for LN-2026-00211 using mortgage_los_get_activity_log (loan_id: "los_loan_223826af9a5d") to discover an empty list -no activity log entries, confirming the Tyler attribution is unverifiable from LOS data.
OE 14: Pull the LOS record for LN-2026-00224 using mortgage_los_get_loan (loan_number: "LN-2026-00224") to discover id=los_loan_48275b0214f9, status="closed" (this is the only one of the three loans the LOS actually shows as closed), loan_type="usda", loan_amount=184000, borrower_id="los_borrower_99d900966886", closing_date="2026-03-10", assigned_processor="los_staff_c4dab6a67e8f", property_address="2581 Johnston Rd, Charlotte, NC 28210", rate_lock_expiration="2026-03-20".
OE 15: Pull the borrower record on LN-2026-00224 using mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id (borrower_id: "los_borrower_99d900966886") to confirm first_name="Steven", last_name="Thomas", email="steven.thomas.224@gmail.com", credit_score=741, annual_income=87000, referral_source="Website".
OE 16: Pull the underwriting condition list for LN-2026-00224 using mortgage_los_get_conditions (loan_id: "los_loan_48275b0214f9") to discover an empty list -no condition records in the LOS.
OE 17: Pull the document checklist for LN-2026-00224 using mortgage_los_get_documents (loan_id: "los_loan_48275b0214f9") to discover 14 items, with drivers_license in status="approved" and received_date=null (the same "approved without received" anomaly seen on LN-2026-00603), and paystub_2 and bank_statement_2 still status="required".
OE 18: Pull the LOS activity log for LN-2026-00224 using mortgage_los_get_activity_log (loan_id: "los_loan_48275b0214f9") to discover an empty list -no activity log entries.
OE 19: Search the LOS borrower table using mortgage_los_search_borrowers (query: "Sullivan" or "Heather Sullivan") to discover Heather Sullivan as los_borrower_0ef58fddd431 (status="closed", address "6923 Sharon Rd, Charlotte, NC 28277", credit_score=656, annual_income=73000, assigned_loan_officer="los_staff_541cdb6e7939"). Heather Sullivan exists in the LOS but is NOT the borrower of record on LN-2026-00603 -the agent must next determine which loan she is actually attached to.
OE 20: Search the LOS loans table using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Sullivan" or "Heather Sullivan") to discover LN-2025-00497 as los_loan_f51f37e91bd5, status="closed", loan_type="conventional", loan_amount=256500, purchase_price=270000, closing_date="2026-01-02", borrower_id="los_borrower_0ef58fddd431", assigned_lo="los_staff_541cdb6e7939", assigned_processor="los_staff_c6b42763fb1f", property_address="6131 Sedgefield Dr, Charlotte, NC 28204", title_company="Commonwealth Land Title". Confirms via LOS that Heather Sullivan's actual loan is the closed 2025 file LN-2025-00497, not LN-2026-00603 as Raymond's March 28 email implied -the cross-loan attribution finding the prompt asks the agent to surface.
OE 21: Search Stripe customers using stripe_search_customers (query: "Heather Sullivan") to discover cus_TpcYNPROqCQpL70a with metadata loan_number="LN-2025-00497", loan_amount=256500, loan_type="Conventional", purchase_price=270000, address="6131 Sedgefield Dr, Charlotte, NC 28204", description="Borrower - LN-2025-00497", corroborating the LOS finding via a second service.
OE 22: Pull Heather Sullivan's full Stripe customer record using stripe_get_customer (customer_id: "cus_TpcYNPROqCQpL70a") to confirm name="Heather Sullivan", email="heather.sullivan@gmail.com", phone="+1-704-465-2156", description="Borrower - LN-2025-00497", metadata loan_number="LN-2025-00497" (NOT LN-2026-00603 as Raymond's March 28 email implied).
OE 23: List Heather Sullivan's Stripe charges using stripe_list_charges (customer_id: "cus_TpcYNPROqCQpL70a") to discover three succeeded charges -ch_d3FHtKPcYe7meXyd ($75 credit report - Sullivan LN-2025-00497), ch_HG61JLs4bEyqtDJw ($800 application deposit - Sullivan LN-2025-00497), and ch_DoBAtpAJnEkhuKHT ($500 appraisal fee - Sullivan LN-2025-00497) -all referencing LN-2025-00497, confirming Raymond's March 28 borrower attribution on LN-2026-00603 was wrong.
OE 24: Search Stripe customers using stripe_search_customers (query: "Janet Torres" or "Torres") for the LOS borrower of record on LN-2026-00603 to discover no Stripe customer matching Janet Torres -a clean negative confirming she has no payment activity tied to a different loan number.
OE 25: Search Stripe customers using stripe_search_customers (query: "Elizabeth Gray" or "Gray") for the LOS borrower of record on LN-2026-00211 to discover no Stripe customer matching Elizabeth Gray -a clean negative confirming the per-borrower payment-activity check returns no cross-loan exposure.
OE 26: Search Stripe customers using stripe_search_customers (query: "Steven Thomas" or "Thomas") for the LOS borrower of record on LN-2026-00224 to discover no Stripe customer matching Steven Thomas -a clean negative completing the per-borrower payment-activity check across all three files.
OE 27: Look up Grace Yamamoto's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Grace Yamamoto") to confirm contact_id="staff_e304643b171b", email="grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com", job="Director of Operations -- Keystone Mortgage Partners".
OE 28: Look up Denise Holloway's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Denise Holloway") to confirm contact_id="staff_a989261d4d33", email="denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com", job="Compliance Officer -- Keystone Mortgage Partners".
OE 29: Add a corrected QC review note to LN-2026-00603 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f", action: "qc_review_note" or similar, author: "raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com") with detail recording the corrected borrower (Janet Torres, not Heather Sullivan), the corrected status (processing, not closed), the empty conditions and activity log finding, the tax_return_prior approved-without-received-date document anomaly, and a reference that the March 28 QC email is being superseded.
OE 30: Add a corrected QC review note to LN-2026-00211 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_223826af9a5d", action: "qc_review_note" or similar, author: "raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com") with detail recording the borrower (Elizabeth Gray), the corrected status (withdrawn, not open), the empty conditions and activity log, the still-required w2_prior and bank_statement_2 documents, and a reference that the March 28 QC email is being superseded.
OE 31: Add a corrected QC review note to LN-2026-00224 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_48275b0214f9", action: "qc_review_note" or similar, author: "raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com") with detail recording that this is the only one of the three loans the LOS actually shows as closed (closing_date 2026-03-10), the borrower (Steven Thomas), the empty conditions and activity log, the drivers_license approved-without-received-date document anomaly, the genuine post-close exposure callout, and a reference that the March 28 QC email is being superseded.
OE 32: Send the corrected exception writeup using send_email (sender: "raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com", "denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com"]) with a subject relating to a corrected/revised QC exception writeup superseding the March 28 summary, and a body containing all three loans with corrected borrower, corrected status, the absence of condition records, the absence of activity log entries, the document anomalies on LN-2026-00603 (tax_return_prior) and LN-2026-00224 (drivers_license), the discovery that LN-2026-00224 is the only loan LOS actually shows closed, and the discovery that Heather Sullivan's payment activity is attached to LN-2025-00497, not LN-2026-00603.
OE 33: Post a heads-up in the #compliance-alerts Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C004") with a payload announcing that a corrected QC exception writeup is going to Grace and Denise to supersede the March 28 summary, noting the borrower-name and status mismatches identified during the re-review.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 19: Search the LOS borrower table using mortgage_los_search_borrowers (query: "Sullivan" or "Heather Sullivan") to discover Heather Sullivan as los_borrower_0ef58fddd431 (status="closed", address "6923 Sharon Rd, Charlotte, NC 28277", credit_score=656, annual_income=73000, assigned_loan_officer="los_staff_541cdb6e7939"). Heather Sullivan exists in the LOS but is NOT the borrower of record on LN-2026-00603 -the agent must next determine which loan she is actually attached to.
OE 20: Search the LOS loans table using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Sullivan" or "Heather Sullivan") to discover LN-2025-00497 as los_loan_f51f37e91bd5, status="closed", loan_type="conventional", loan_amount=256500, purchase_price=270000, closing_date="2026-01-02", borrower_id="los_borrower_0ef58fddd431", assigned_lo="los_staff_541cdb6e7939", assigned_processor="los_staff_c6b42763fb1f", property_address="6131 Sedgefield Dr, Charlotte, NC 28204", title_company="Commonwealth Land Title". Confirms via LOS that Heather Sullivan's actual loan is the closed 2025 file LN-2025-00497, not LN-2026-00603 as Raymond's March 28 email implied -the cross-loan attribution finding the prompt asks the agent to surface.
OE 21: Search Stripe customers using stripe_search_customers (query: "Heather Sullivan") to discover cus_TpcYNPROqCQpL70a with metadata loan_number="LN-2025-00497", loan_amount=256500, loan_type="Conventional", purchase_price=270000, address="6131 Sedgefield Dr, Charlotte, NC 28204", description="Borrower - LN-2025-00497", corroborating the LOS finding via a second service.
OE 22: Pull Heather Sullivan's full Stripe customer record using stripe_get_customer (customer_id: "cus_TpcYNPROqCQpL70a") to confirm name="Heather Sullivan", email="heather.sullivan@gmail.com", phone="+1-704-465-2156", description="Borrower - LN-2025-00497", metadata loan_number="LN-2025-00497" (NOT LN-2026-00603 as Raymond's March 28 email implied).
OE 23: List Heather Sullivan's Stripe charges using stripe_list_charges (customer_id: "cus_TpcYNPROqCQpL70a") to discover three succeeded charges -ch_d3FHtKPcYe7meXyd ($75 credit report - Sullivan LN-2025-00497), ch_HG61JLs4bEyqtDJw ($800 application deposit - Sullivan LN-2025-00497), and ch_DoBAtpAJnEkhuKHT ($500 appraisal fee - Sullivan LN-2025-00497) -all referencing LN-2025-00497, confirming Raymond's March 28 borrower attribution on LN-2026-00603 was wrong.
OE 24: Search Stripe customers using stripe_search_customers (query: "Janet Torres" or "Torres") for the LOS borrower of record on LN-2026-00603 to discover no Stripe customer matching Janet Torres -a clean negative confirming she has no payment activity tied to a different loan number.
OE 25: Search Stripe customers using stripe_search_customers (query: "Elizabeth Gray" or "Gray") for the LOS borrower of record on LN-2026-00211 to discover no Stripe customer matching Elizabeth Gray -a clean negative confirming the per-borrower payment-activity check returns no cross-loan exposure.
OE 26: Search Stripe customers using stripe_search_customers (query: "Steven Thomas" or "Thomas") for the LOS borrower of record on LN-2026-00224 to discover no Stripe customer matching Steven Thomas -a clean negative completing the per-borrower payment-activity check across all three files.
OE 27: Look up Grace Yamamoto's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Grace Yamamoto") to confirm contact_id="staff_e304643b171b", email="grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com", job="Director of Operations -- Keystone Mortgage Partners".
OE 28: Look up Denise Holloway's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Denise Holloway") to confirm contact_id="staff_a989261d4d33", email="denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com", job="Compliance Officer -- Keystone Mortgage Partners".
OE 29: Add a corrected QC review note to LN-2026-00603 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_ff737c0f7a8f", action: "qc_review_note" or similar, author: "raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com") with detail recording the corrected borrower (Janet Torres, not Heather Sullivan), the corrected status (processing, not closed), the empty conditions and activity log finding, the tax_return_prior approved-without-received-date document anomaly, and a reference that the March 28 QC email is being superseded.
OE 30: Add a corrected QC review note to LN-2026-00211 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_223826af9a5d", action: "qc_review_note" or similar, author: "raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com") with detail recording the borrower (Elizabeth Gray), the corrected status (withdrawn, not open), the empty conditions and activity log, the still-required w2_prior and bank_statement_2 documents, and a reference that the March 28 QC email is being superseded.
OE 31: Add a corrected QC review note to LN-2026-00224 using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_48275b0214f9", action: "qc_review_note" or similar, author: "raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com") with detail recording that this is the only one of the three loans the LOS actually shows as closed (closing_date 2026-03-10), the borrower (Steven Thomas), the empty conditions and activity log, the drivers_license approved-without-received-date document anomaly, the genuine post-close exposure callout, and a reference that the March 28 QC email is being superseded.
OE 32: Send the corrected exception writeup using send_email (sender: "raymond.chen@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com", "denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com"]) with a subject relating to a corrected/revised QC exception writeup superseding the March 28 summary, and a body containing all three loans with corrected borrower, corrected status, the absence of condition records, the absence of activity log entries, the document anomalies on LN-2026-00603 (tax_return_prior) and LN-2026-00224 (drivers_license), the discovery that LN-2026-00224 is the only loan LOS actually shows closed, and the discovery that Heather Sullivan's payment activity is attached to LN-2025-00497, not LN-2026-00603.
OE 33: Post a heads-up in the #compliance-alerts Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C004") with a payload announcing that a corrected QC exception writeup is going to Grace and Denise to supersede the March 28 summary, noting the borrower-name and status mismatches identified during the re-review.

14 The email to Grace and Denise (visible in `send_email` content parameter) reports the cross-system finding that Heather Sullivan's payment activity on file is tied to a different loan number, LN-2025-00497 (not LN-2026-00603).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✗✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The email's CROSS-LOAN PAYMENT CHECK section explicitly states: 'Heather Sullivan... has three charges in Stripe... but all are tied to her actual loan, LN-2025-00497.' Both Heather Sullivan and LN-2025-00497 are referenced together.
✓Run #2The email explicitly states 'Sullivan IS a real borrower but on a completely different loan — LN-2025-00497' and notes her Stripe payment activity is all tied to LN-2025-00497.

✗Run #4The email does not mention Heather Sullivan or LN-2025-00497. The agent searched Stripe for 'Torres' charges (the LOS borrower) and found loan numbers LN-2024-00178, LN-2025-00343, and LN-2025-00511. The specific cross-system finding of Heather Sullivan's payment activity tied to LN-2025-00497 was never discovered or reported.

19 The email to Grace and Denise (visible in `send_email` content parameter) contains an explicit answer to Grace's third follow-up question: whether this is a Tyler-only problem or a normalized control gap. The answer must render an explicit position (or similar), not just list facts without drawing a conclusion.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email contains a 'BROADER CONTROL GAP QUESTION' section that renders an explicit position: 'what the system of record shows is not a condition-clearing control gap. It shows a different set of problems... I would not frame corrective action around condition clearance based on what the files actually show.' This is an explicit analytical conclusion, not just a fact dump.
✓Run #2The email renders an explicit position: 'That looks more like a systemic process control issue than an individual actor problem' while noting the Tyler question can't be answered since Tyler Washington doesn't exist in the system.
✓Run #3The email explicitly answers: 'Tyler-only vs. control gap: Two of three files had inactive/terminated processors...This pattern suggests the doc gap issue is not limited to Tyler's condition clearances -- it extends to files where the assigned staff are no longer with the company and the handoff/audit trail was not maintained.' This is an explicit position, not just a fact dump.
✓Run #4The email takes an explicit position on the Tyler vs. control gap question: 'These point to systemic file management and staffing controls, not a single-person conditions issue' — rendering a clear conclusion that this is a normalized control gap, not a Tyler-only problem.
✓Run #5The email explicitly answers Grace's Q3: 'This looks more like a gap in file handoff and condition-tracking controls than an individual actor problem.' This renders an explicit position (normalized control gap, not Tyler-only).
✓Run #6The email answers Grace's third question with an explicit position: 'The actual issues... point to operational control gaps that are broader than any single person. These are systemic issues around processor assignment, document management, and compliance date tracking.' This renders a clear conclusion (systemic/normalized gap, not Tyler-only).
